nodes.label: Token Model (JWT 15 min access + opaque 30-day refresh)
nodes.rationale: Short-lived RS256/EdDSA JWT access tokens limit the exposure window and carry exactly sub, role, device_id so requests can be authorized and scoped without a database round trip; refresh tokens are opaque (not JWTs) precisely so they can be stored hashed at rest — the backend only ever compares hashes. Exactly one active refresh token per device; both tokens stored only in Keychain on clients.

nodes.label: Refresh-Token Rotation with Family Reuse Detection
nodes.rationale: Every refresh rotates the token within the same family; reuse of an already-rotated token is treated as theft and revokes the entire family for that device, so a stolen refresh token is usable at most once before detection bounds the blast radius.

nodes.label: Sign in with Apple (primary auth)
nodes.rationale: Identity token verified against Apple's JWKS (iss/aud/exp validated); accounts are linked by the token's sub claim stored as apple_user_id, never by email alone, because Apple-provided emails can be relay addresses and are not a stable key. Email/password (argon2id: 64 MiB, 3 iterations, parallelism 4) is secondary; both converge on the same token model.

nodes.label: RBAC (user/admin, user_id scoping)
nodes.rationale: Every query is scoped by user_id from the access token's sub claim at the data-access layer — not left to individual handlers — ruling out cross-tenant access paths; admin routes live under /v1/admin gated by role middleware checking role == admin.

nodes.label: Rate Limiting (token bucket, per-IP auth / per-user sync)
nodes.rationale: Per-IP on /v1/auth/* (suggested 10/min) to blunt credential stuffing; per-user on /v1/sync/* and /v1/reports/* (suggested 60/min) sized for client cadence; brute-force lockout per account is additionally required so attackers cannot evade per-IP limits by distributing across IPs.

nodes.label: GDPR Data Lifecycle (7-day grace, export, 72h breach)
nodes.rationale: DELETE /v1/users/me creates a deletion_requests row and starts a 7-day grace period before a background job hard-cascade-deletes all user data; POST /v1/users/me/export produces a full JSON dump for portability; breach notification within 72 hours.

nodes.label: Certificate Pinning Deliberately Deferred
nodes.rationale: Deferred, not omitted by oversight: pinning adds meaningful operational risk (a mismanaged pin rotation can hard-lock every client out until an app update ships) for marginal gain over TLS 1.2+ and HSTS against the current threat model; revisit if the threat model changes.
